title: Boccherini editions manifest
description: Contract between boccherini-quartet-data (producer) and boccherini-sampler (consumer). For every quartet it enumerates the available editions and their per-part completeness facts, so the sampler can assemble and verify bundles WITHOUT re-deriving anything from filenames or page content. Catalog metadata (title, key, movements, year, links) is NOT duplicated here — join to opera.json by `gerard`.

schema.description: Schema id + version. Bump on any breaking change.

generated.description: ISO-8601 date the manifest was produced (informational).

parts_base.description: Base URL or filesystem path that each part `file` is resolved against. Empty/absent means `file` values are already resolvable on their own (absolute path/URL, or relative to the manifest).

works.description: Map of Gérard catalog number (as a string) -> work.

gerard.description: Gérard number; MUST equal the map key.

opus.description: Opus number — convenience/sanity only; the catalog truth is opera.json.

number.description: Work number within the opus (Op.X No.Y), if the work has one.

editions.description: All known editions/sources for this work. Order is not significant; the sampler chooses which to use (edition preference is a sampler input, never hardcoded here).

id.description: Stable slug, unique within the work (e.g. 'philhar-lilypond', 'km-scan', 'sieber').

label.description: Human-readable edition name for the UI (e.g. 'Philhar / LilyPond', 'KM (engraving scan)').

form.description: per-work-parts: each part resolves to its own PDF. opus-bundle: parts are page-ranges inside multi-work files (every part MUST set source_pages). score-only: a full score exists but no separated parts (parts has only 'score').

parts.description: Map of part label -> unit. Only the parts that actually exist appear (an edition may be missing a part, or be score-only).

part.description: One atomic, COMPLETE part. The sampler's completeness invariant: the resolved PDF (or the extracted source_pages range) has exactly `pages` pages. This is what makes 'no missing pages' checkable for any source, scans included.

file.description: Path/URL to the source PDF, resolved against parts_base.

pages.description: Authoritative complete page count of this part. THE completeness fact.

source_pages.description: 1-based inclusive [start, end] range within `file` to extract, when this part lives inside a multi-work bundle. (end - start + 1) MUST equal `pages`. Omit when `file` is already a standalone part.

sha256.description: Optional SHA-256 of the resolved part (whole file or extracted range) for drift detection between the two repos.

provenance.description: Where this edition came from and its nature — for attribution, licensing, and trust.

source.description: Human description and/or URL of origin (IMSLP work page, library, archive).

text.description: true if the PDF has extractable text (boundaries are machine-checkable); false for image-only scans, where the declared `pages` count is the only completeness signal.
